title: mentha sylvestris subvar. hispidula
intro: Mentha sylvestris subvar. hispidula, also known as the Wild Mint or Hispid Wood Mint, is a lesser-known subspecies of the common mint plant, characterized by its distinctive hispid (hair-like) leaves. The plant is rich in essential oils, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties, such as analgesic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Mentha sylvestris subvar. hispidula include its ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate headaches and nausea, and provide relief from respiratory problems like coughs and colds. This plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be grown in gardens or wildcrafted for its medicinal and culinary uses.
